title: Thai-Style Sirloin Stir Fry
description: A flavorful and spicy stir fry dish made with sirloin, fermented Thai chilies, and a hint of creamy coconut milk.
instructions: Heat 1 tablespoon of vegetable oil in a large skillet or wok over medium-high heat.
Add the garlic and sauté for 1-2 minutes until fragrant.
Add the sirloin and cook for 3-4 minutes per side, or until it reaches your desired level of doneness.
Transfer the sirloin to a plate and cover with foil to keep warm.
In the same skillet, add the remaining 1 tablespoon of vegetable oil, the fermented Thai chilies, and the stew.
Cook for 2-3 minutes, stirring constantly, until the stew is heated through.
Stir in the coconut milk and bring the mixture to a simmer.
Return the sirloin to the skillet and spoon some of the sauce over the top.
Reduce the heat to low and simmer for 2-3 minutes, or until the sirloin is coated in the sauce.
Serve the sirloin over fluffy white rice, garnished with fresh cilantro if desired.
Season with Thai spices and fish sauce to taste.
